title: Major Outlets Report Iran Nearing Economic Collapse
detail: Time magazine, Euronews, and Al-Monitor publish concurrent analyses declaring Iran's economy is approaching collapse. Time's cover story cites the rial at 1,750,000 per USD (175,000 tomans), a 35% decline since the June Twelve-Day War. All three reports identify the convergence of snapback sanctions, war costs, and subsidy removal as creating an unprecedented fiscal crisis.

title: Finance Minister Blames War for Rial Collapse
detail: Iran's Minister of Economy and Finance publicly attributes the rial's historic decline to the costs of the June 2025 Twelve-Day War with Israel, stating the conflict 'burned through $8 billion in reserves and shattered investor confidence.' The rare admission contradicts the Supreme Leader's narrative of economic resilience and triggers a sharp rebuke from hardline media outlets.

title: ICG Warns of Imminent Social Unrest in Iran
detail: The International Crisis Group publishes an urgent briefing warning that Iran's economic deterioration has created conditions for large-scale social unrest. The report notes that working-class Iranians are spending over 70% of household income on food, energy costs are spiraling, and public anger at the regime's war spending is reaching a tipping point not seen since the 2022 Mahsa Amini protests.
